title: Update notes-api deployment spec with GHCR-based CI/CD
description:
The current notes-api-deployment.md spec describes a v1 'clone repo on VPS, build inside Docker' workflow. The spec itself flags this as the 'solo, single host' shortcut and calls out the graduation triggers (slow Rust rebuilds, staging parity, contributor snowflake) in the 'When this stops being right' section.

We've decided to skip the git-clone-and-build phase and go straight to a more standard CI/CD setup. Update the spec to describe:

**Pipeline:**
1. GitHub Actions builds Docker image on push to main (use docker/build-push-action with registry-backed layer caching)
2. Push to GHCR (ghcr.io/vonshlovens/notes-api:sha-<short> + :latest)
3. Final job opens Tailscale (tailscale/github-action@v2 with OAuth client) and SSHes to the VPS
4. VPS runs 'docker compose pull api admin && docker compose up -d'

**Portability discipline (so we can leave GitHub later without rewriting everything):**
- Build/deploy logic lives in scripts/build.sh and scripts/deploy.sh in the repo
- The CI YAML is a thin caller — checkout, login, run scripts
- GHCR is fine for now; it's just an OCI registry and works with Forgejo/Gitea later if we ever self-host
- Migration story to alternative CI (Forgejo Actions, Woodpecker, Blacksmith runners) becomes a YAML rewrite, not a pipeline re-architecture

**What stays from current spec:**
- docker-compose.yml, Caddy + Tailscale Serve, Postgres/Garage volumes
- Migrations as a one-shot via 'docker compose run --rm api sqlx migrate run'
- Secrets stay on the VPS in .env / docker secrets (CI doesn't ship secrets, only images)
- Rollback story (re-run old workflow OR check out old SHA on VPS and pull that tag)

**What changes:**
- 'build: ./api' / 'build: ./admin' in compose become 'image: ghcr.io/...'
- VPS no longer needs cargo or Rust toolchain
- 'Workflow' section in the spec gets rewritten; 'When this stops being right' section gets removed (we're skipping the v1)
- Add a 'CI/CD' section with the workflow YAML sketch

Out of scope for this issue: self-hosting git. We're staying on GitHub for the foreseeable future. The portability discipline above is enough to keep that door open without doing the work now.

title: Fix Codex Beads skills/plugin availability
description: Codex sessions can read the project Beads instructions and use the bd CLI, but no dedicated Beads skill or plugin is currently exposed through tool discovery. Add or repair the Codex Beads skill/plugin so agents can discover and use Beads capabilities directly, including guidance for this repo's remote Railway Dolt setup.
acceptance_criteria: A Beads skill or plugin is discoverable in Codex sessions; it documents the remote Railway Dolt server behavior and the rule not to run bd dolt push/pull; agents can create/update/close beads through the supported workflow without relying only on AGENTS.md.

title: Wire blog /writing and /writing/[slug] to notes-api
description: Replace the hardcoded src/lib/content/hello-world.ts fixture with calls to GET /v1/notes and GET /v1/notes/article/:slug from +page.server.ts. Configure tailscale-railway-bridge so Railway can dial the Tailnet hostname. Per specs/notes.md (Blog ↔ notes-api integration).

title: Author notes/docker-compose.yml + Caddyfile + bootstrap scripts
description: docker-compose.yml with caddy, postgres (pgvector/pgvector:pg18), garage, api, admin services per specs/notes-api-deployment.md. Caddyfile routing /api/v1/* and /attach/* and / to the right backends. notes/scripts/bootstrap-garage.sh for first-run cluster + bucket setup. .env.example with all required vars.

title: Build notes/plugin/ (Obsidian) — port + simplify obsync-pg-plugin
description: Port watcher, debouncer, parser, hasher modules from obsync-pg-plugin to notes/plugin/. Replace the Postgres client with an HTTP client to /v1/sync/*. Implement push with CAS, pull via /v1/sync/changes cursor, and conflict UX (sidecar files). Settings tab + status panel. Build pipeline produces single main.js (no vendored node_modules). Per specs/notes-plugin.md.

title: Implement public read endpoints in notes-api (blog consumer)
description: GET /v1/notes, /v1/notes/:type/:slug (with attachment URL rewriting and link resolution), /v1/notes/:type/:slug/backlinks, /v1/tags. Anonymous, returns only publish=true AND deleted_at IS NULL rows. Per specs/notes-sync-api.md and specs/notes.md (render rules).

title: Implement /v1/sync/* endpoints in notes-api with CAS
description: Implement PUT /v1/sync/notes (with expected_revision CAS), DELETE /v1/sync/notes, GET /v1/sync/manifest, GET /v1/sync/changes?since=<rev>, plus attachment endpoints. Bearer-token auth middleware, JSON error envelope per specs/notes-sync-api.md. Includes integration tests against a real Postgres via testcontainers.

title: Author notes-api Postgres migrations (notes, change_log, attachments_metadata, pgvector)
description: Initial sqlx migration creating pgcrypto + vector extensions, the notes table with embedding column, change_log with BIGSERIAL revision, attachments_metadata, and all indexes per specs/notes-data-model.md. Migration is idempotent and runs cleanly on a fresh Postgres 18.

title: Stand up notes-api Rust+Axum service skeleton
description: Initial Rust+Axum service in notes/api/ per specs/notes-sync-api.md. Cargo workspace, sqlx, tracing, /healthz + /readyz, Dockerfile. No business logic yet — just the shell that connects to Postgres and Garage and answers health checks.

title: Build archive/index page per docs/init-design.md
description: Implement the archive (post index) page following the design prototype in docs/init-design.md (around lines 510-750). Grid/list of all posts with archive_stats sidebar and row-based article entries. Needs SvelteKit route, content loader that lists all posts, and filtering/sorting hooks to extend later.

title: Build article page per docs/init-design.md
description: Implement the single-article page following the design prototype in docs/init-design.md (around lines 350-500). This is a core reader experience — article header, body with typography/rhythm, related-article tiles, and footer. Needs SvelteKit route, layout, and typography styles consistent with the existing design system scaffolded on the homepage.

title: Add animation specification
description:
Standardize animation across the project using best-in-class tooling for SvelteKit 5.

Goals:
- Define a single source of truth for motion tokens (durations, easings, spring configs)
- Choose primary animation primitives (Svelte built-in transitions, motion-one / Motion for Svelte, CSS) and document when to use each
- Establish patterns for: page transitions, component enter/exit, micro-interactions, scroll-triggered animations, reduced-motion handling
- Produce a spec in specs/ that future features can reference

Deliverables:
- specs/animation.md with tokens, primitives, and usage patterns
- Example references in code if helpful

title: Implement attachment storage path (Garage S3) in notes-api
description: PUT /v1/sync/attachments multipart handler that verifies SHA256, deduplicates by content-hash, uploads to Garage at attachments/{sha256}/{filename}, writes attachments_metadata row. /attach/* served via Caddy reverse proxy with immutable cache headers. Soft-delete + 90-day GC job. Per specs/notes-storage.md.

title: Build notes/admin/ SvelteKit admin browser
description: SvelteKit app in notes/admin/. Tailscale identity auth via header. Routes: /, /notes, /notes/:id, /notes/:id/preview, /search, /tags, /conflicts. Calls /v1/admin/* on the Rust API. Reuses Tailwind v4 + bits-ui from the blog. Per specs/notes-admin.md.

title: Comments feature on published blogs/notes
description: Implement the actual commenting UI and backend on published /writing entries and notes: submit, list, thread (if in scope), edit/delete own comments, and moderation hooks. Depends on auth providers, sign-in flow, rate limiting, profanity filter, and profile infrastructure being in place.

title: Reader profile settings
description: Let signed-in readers edit their profile: display name, avatar, notification preferences, and account deletion. Needed so commenters can control how they appear on the site.

title: Reader profile page
description: Public profile page per reader showing display name, avatar, and their comment history. Needed so a comment byline can link somewhere meaningful.

title: Profanity / abuse filter for comments
description: Filter or flag comments containing profanity and abusive content before they're published. Decide on library vs. service and on hard-reject vs. soft-flag-for-review policy.

title: Rate limiting for user-generated content
description: Add rate limiting around comment submission (and any other UGC endpoints) to prevent spam and abuse. Likely per-user + per-IP limits with sensible defaults.

title: Improve authenticated sign-in flow
description: Build a polished sign-in experience for readers on top of the configured auth providers: sign-in UI, session handling, sign-out, and protecting commenting routes. Prerequisite for letting readers comment.

title: Set up third-party auth providers (Google, GitHub)
description: Integrate OAuth sign-in with Google and GitHub so readers can authenticate without bespoke account creation. Needed before comments so commenters have a durable identity tied to a real provider account.

title: Epic: Comments on published blogs/notes
description: Enable readers to leave comments on published writing (blogs and notes). This is a substantial feature that depends on several prerequisite systems: proper authenticated sign-in with third-party providers, abuse mitigation (rate limiting + profanity filter), and reader profile infrastructure (profile pages + settings). Track as an epic with child issues for each prerequisite plus the comment feature itself.

title: Spec out Obsidian notes integration
description: Design (not implement) how Obsidian vault notes flow into the blog. Decide on sync mechanism (git submodule vs. export script vs. live pull), frontmatter conventions, link/backlink handling, attachment/image pipeline, draft vs. published gating, and how notes surface alongside articles on the site. Deliverable: a spec in specs/ plus any ADRs needed. No code.
close_reason: Specs delivered: specs/notes.md + 6 sub-specs at specs/notes-*.md, plus 5 ADRs at notes/docs/adr/. Implementation tracked in von-elx, von-16s, von-2z3, von-dc9, von-wmt, von-0v4, von-jz4, von-cnj, von-09v.

value: Notes-api VPS is the Hostinger box at 157.173.212.195, on Tailnet as 'notes' (100.65.47.66, formerly 'supa'). Ubuntu 24.04, Docker + compose v2.40.3 ready, Tailscale 1.96.4. Reach via 'ssh notes' from the laptop (uses ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_notes via SSH config). Linux hostname still says 'supa' — only Tailscale hostname was renamed; cosmetic only. Deploy directory: /opt/vonshlovens (empty).
